task_statement: P7d3 of BRICK 6-surface audit repair: same corrected barrier shape as P7d2, plus an honest COO-declared sibling_independence vouch on the lane-qa-fanin group. P7d2 (0701e) reached frontier=link_paused after a legitimate reroute on work-docs (closure boundary_mismatch concern, honestly targeting the real producer of the mis-attributed changed_files): docs-lane-qa re-verified clean, but its sibling checker-lane-qa still held its original attempt-1 result, and the lane-qa-fanin-confirm barrier correctly refused to silently accept a stale sibling without a declared vouch (support/operator/walker_kernel.py:2036-2075, "absent => re-verify all siblings (conservative)"). work-docs (README.md, quickstart.md, setup.md, launch-guide.md, architecture-map.md only) and work-checker (support/operator/cli.py, support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py, support/checkers/profiles/brick_cli_entrypoint.yaml only) have completely disjoint declared write_scope allowed_paths -- a reroute confined to one lane genuinely cannot invalidate the other lane's prior QA pass. This Building declares that fact openly on the lane-qa-fanin group via sibling_independence, per support/operator/plan_graph.py:276-295 ("a HUMAN-declared vouch ... Support READS this declaration; it NEVER synthesizes independence"). This is NOT an instruction to any Agent to misreport a reroute target -- QA/closure concerns must still name the real node they observed a defect in; this vouch only controls whether an UNRELATED sibling with disjoint write_scope must be re-verified after a reroute on the OTHER lane.
selection_rule: manual graph because S6-F4 requires the big-work route shape itself: task intake -> design fan-out -> design synthesis/plan confirm -> parallel work lanes -> lane QA fan-in -> lane-qa-fanin-confirm barrier -> final QA fan-out -> closure. A linear preset would not preserve the phase contract. P7d2 corrects the P7d (0701d) shape where lane QA fanned directly into three final-QA fan-in targets with no barrier between the fan-in and the next fan-out.

proof_limits: sibling_independence vouch is a COO-declared fact about disjoint write_scope, not agent-reported evidence
does not vouch semantic correctness of either lane, only that one lane cannot invalidate the other
not_proven: whether future edits to either lane could break the disjoint write_scope assumption

proof_limits: official brick build graph evidence is support evidence only
P7d2 closes only the docs/route-wording + checker-pin part of S6-F4 Easy Building declaration ergonomics, if proven

P7d2 does not prove P8 ship safety, P9 dynamic customer replay, fresh-machine install/run, real-provider reliability, customer comprehension, source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority
Claude design lane is graph-level design fan-out evidence only; provider quality and future reliability remain not_proven
not_proven: full external brick build --graph <packet> dynamic-design ergonomics pipeline proof (explicitly deferred to a later slice, matching P7b/P7c task scoping)
whole-repo check_profile.py --all green independent of this slice's own changed files
